recommendations: add a minimal smoke-test suite for the MCP plugin (server startup
one import-based call
one CLI-fallback call)
pin and validate the yt compatibility range in `mcp_output/requirements.txt` (or document tested versions) to reduce breakage from upstream API shifts
add structured error mapping in `adapter.py` (import errors
dataset load errors
field lookup errors) with actionable messages
implement lazy imports for heavy visualization/volume-rendering endpoints to improve startup latency and memory use
add endpoint-level timeouts and cancellation guards for long-running loads/renders
introduce input validation schemas for each endpoint (paths
field names
geometry params) to fail fast and safely
add a capability-discovery endpoint that reports optional dependency availability (matplotlib/scipy/h5py/etc.) and disables unsupported tools dynamically
create a focused regression test matrix for high-risk core surfaces (`loaders`
`Dataset`
selection containers
plotting classes)
add observability hooks (structured logs + per-endpoint timing + failure counters) in `mcp_service.py`
separate “stable” vs “experimental” endpoints in README and metadata to set user expectations
document frontend-specific caveats and required extras for common dataset types in `README_MCP.md`
add a lightweight benchmark script for representative calls (load + sliceplot + profileplot) and track baseline in CI
enforce type checking (mypy/pyright) on the MCP code and tighten pydantic models for request/response payloads
add safe resource cleanup patterns (closing datasets/files
temporary artifact cleanup) for long-lived MCP processes
include a fallback/degradation strategy when DeepWiki or external analysis metadata is unavailable so plugin generation remains deterministic

success_factors: Workflow reached terminal success state with all planned nodes executed (download, analysis, env, generate, run, review, finalize).
Import-first adapter strategy was appropriate for yt’s API-rich Python architecture (import feasibility 0.86, strategy confidence 0.89).
Generated MCP service started healthy over stdio and passed plugin-level run validation.
Core module targeting was sensible (loaders, Dataset, selection containers, plotting, scene), giving broad initial coverage.
failure_reasons: Quality gaps remain: original project tests were not executed successfully, DeepWiki analysis failed, and generated artifact metrics (LOC/size/endpoints) appear inconsistent with service info.
Test validation depth is low (startup health only), so functional correctness risk remains for complex yt operations.

node_performance.download_time: Completed successfully via zip fallback; repository is large (~926 files), so this likely consumed a meaningful portion of the 234.97s total.
node_performance.analysis_time: Static/package analysis completed with useful module mapping and risk scoring; DeepWiki sub-step failed but did not block pipeline progress.
node_performance.generation_time: Code generation completed quickly and produced expected MCP scaffold files plus import adapter and README.
node_performance.test_time: Plugin runtime health check passed, but no substantial endpoint smoke/regression suite was executed; effective test depth is minimal.
